name: Atlantic Route
name_original: Praia de Santa Cruz — percurso circular
description: Hike the Praia de Santa Cruz circular route, a moderate 5.4-mile coastal path with stunning cliff-top views near Lisbon.
region_intro: Easy hiking trails around Silveira, Portugal, are characterized by their coastal and rural landscapes. The region features trails along the Alcabrichel River and extends towards the Atlantic coast, offering diverse scenery. Hikers can expect paths that traverse beaches, cliffs, and areas with natural features like the Penedo do Guincho rock formation. The terrain generally includes gentle elevation changes, making it suitable for accessible outdoor activities. Best easy hiking trails around Silveira The most popular easy hiking route is Praia Azul – View from the Cliff loop from Largo da Foz, a 2.6 miles (4.2 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 9 minutes to complete. This route offers coastal views and passes by the Praia Azul beach. Another top favourite among local hikers is Foz do Sizandro – Praia Azul loop from Largo da Foz, an easy 2.7 miles (4.4 km) path. This trail provides a combination of river estuary scenery and coastal stretches near Praia Azul. Local hikers also love the Santa Cruz Beach – Guincho Rock loop from Aeroclube de Torres Vedras, a 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail leading through coastal areas and past the Penedo do Guincho rock, often completed in about 1 hour 12 minutes. Hiking around Silveira is defined by coastal paths, riverine landscapes, and beaches. The network offers options for various ability levels, with 13 easy hiking trails available. The routes in Silveira are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 130 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Silveira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available in Silveira?
A: There are 13 easy hiking trails around Silveira, offering a variety of coastal and rural landscapes. In total, the region features 32 hiking routes across all difficulty levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on easy hikes in Silveira?
A: Easy hikes in Silveira feature gentle elevation changes and diverse scenery. You can expect paths that traverse beaches, cliffs, and areas along the Alcabrichel River, offering a mix of coastal and rural environments.

Q: Are there any easy circular walks in Silveira?
A: Yes, many easy trails in Silveira are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32166493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia Azul – View from the Cliff loop from Largo da Foz&lt;/a&gt; is a 4.2 km (2.6 miles) loop offering coastal views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34349772" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz do Sizandro – Praia Azul loop from Largo da Foz&lt;/a&gt;, which combines river estuary scenery with coastal stretches.

Q: What are some interesting natural features or landmarks to see on easy hikes?
A: Along the easy trails, you can discover several natural features. The impressive 30-meter high &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/628971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penedo do Guincho&lt;/a&gt; rock formation is a notable landmark near Santa Cruz Beach. You can also enjoy views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3538591" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto da Vela Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/404970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rio Alcabrichel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the easy hiking trails in Silveira suitable for families?
A: Yes, the gentle elevation changes and diverse scenery make many easy trails in Silveira suitable for families. The coastal paths and beaches provide engaging environments for all ages. Consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32168304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Cruz Beach – Guincho Rock loop from Aeroclube de Torres Vedras&lt;/a&gt; for a family-friendly coastal experience.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the easy hiking trails in Silveira?
A: Many trails in Silveira are dog-friendly, especially those in natural, open areas. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially near wildlife or other hikers. The coastal and riverine paths generally offer good opportunities for walks with your canine companion.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Silveira?
A: Silveira benefits from a mild climate, making it pleasant for hiking year-round. Spring and summer offer vibrant flora and fauna, while autumn brings changing foliage. Even winter provides a tranquil atmosphere for outdoor activities, particularly on the easy trails.

Q: Are there any short easy hikes for a quick outing?
A: Yes, there are several short and easy options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32166493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia Azul – View from the Cliff loop from Largo da Foz&lt;/a&gt; is just 4.2 km (2.6 miles) and takes about 1 hour and 9 minutes. Another similar option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32156538" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia Azul – View from the Cliff loop from Aeroclube de Torres Vedras&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.9 km (3 miles).

Q: What do other hikers say about the easy trails in Silveira?
A: The easy trails in Silveira are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 130 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse coastal and riverine landscapes, as well as the accessibility of the paths.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the easy hiking routes?
A: Yes, several easy routes offer scenic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3538591" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto da Vela Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; provides expansive views, and many coastal trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32170063" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz do Sizandro – View from the Cliff loop from Largo da Foz&lt;/a&gt;, offer picturesque vistas of the Atlantic Ocean and the coastline.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the trailheads in Silveira?
A: While specific public transport options to every trailhead may vary, the region around Silveira in Torres Vedras is generally accessible. For routes starting near Praia Azul or Foz do Sizandro, local bus services might connect to nearby towns. It's advisable to check local transport schedules for the most up-to-date information.

Q: Are there parking facilities near the easy hiking trails?
A: Yes, parking is generally available near popular trailheads and beaches in Silveira. For example, routes starting from Largo da Foz or Aeroclube de Torres Vedras typically have designated parking areas, making it convenient for those traveling by car.
tips: Santa Cruz, with its extensive sand divided into several beaches, is famous for the purity of its waters and sand and the beauty of its picturesque rocks. Penedo do Guincho stands out, an impressive 30 meter high rock. From the top of the terraces, it is possible to admire a vast panorama, including the Cabo Carvoeiro lighthouse and the Berlengas Islands. The region is well equipped with infrastructure, including a swimming pool and even an Olympic shooting pit.
At the north end of Santa Cruz is one of the calmest spots on the beach, right in front of the cliff. Large rocks on the sand.
The trail over the cliff offers a complete panorama of Santa Cruz beach. Very quiet place, taking into account that it is located close to Lisbon.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Praia de Santa Cruz — circular route?
faqs.answer: The trail is located in A Dos Cunhados, Lisbon, Portugal. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, you can typically find parking near the Santa Cruz beach area, which serves as a good starting point for this circular route.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered dog-friendly, offering a great coastal walk for you and your pet. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially along cliff-top paths and in areas where wildlife might be present.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking. These seasons also provide beautiful coastal scenery without the peak summer crowds or winter's potentially rougher weather.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to hike the Praia de Santa Cruz — circular route. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the circular route?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily runs along cliff tops, offering easily accessible paths. You'll experience a varied coastline with small coves, and broader areas featuring dune systems and cliffs. The path provides a complete panorama of Santa Cruz beach.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy stunning panoramic views of Santa Cruz beach from the cliff-top path. Notable highlights include Penedo do Guincho, Santa Cruz Beach, the impressive Cliffs and Rock Formations of Santa Cruz Beach, and the scenic Santa Cruz Beach and Waterfall.

faqs.question: Can I reach the trailhead using public transport?
faqs.answer: While the trail is close to Lisbon, specific public transport connections directly to the trailhead are not detailed. It's advisable to check local bus routes or consider driving to the Santa Cruz area for easier access.

faqs.question: Is the Praia de Santa Cruz circular route suitable for families?
faqs.answer: Rated as moderate, the route requires good fitness but its paths are mostly accessible. With a distance of about 8.7 kilometers and an estimated duration of 2 hours and 16 minutes, it's suitable for families with a reasonable level of fitness who are used to hiking.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route is part of a larger network. It intersects with sections of official trails such as the Caminho do Atlântico - Rede Natura do Oeste, Rota do Atlântico, and the EuroVelo 1 - Atlantic Coast Route.

faqs.question: What kind of experience can I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: You can expect a quiet and serene experience with stunning panoramic coastal views. Despite its proximity to Lisbon, the area is known for its tranquility, offering a peaceful escape with a blend of natural landscapes including sea and potentially mountain views.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restrooms nearby?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near Santa Cruz beach, which is a developed area. You can typically find cafes, restaurants, and other amenities in the vicinity of the beach, making it convenient for pre- or post-hike refreshments.
